catalyst: Free cash flow conversion recovering above 80% as whatever suppressed FY2024-25 cash generation washes through
reasoning: No disqualifying stage failed. Type A means a Stage 2 failure would be an automatic Avoid, and this passes only narrowly: worldwide unit case volume was even at 33.8 billion cases and concentrate volume grew 1%, so volume is not negative. But the composition is what Bedrock's anti-inflation filter exists to expose. Of 2% revenue growth, price/mix contributed 4% and volume 1%. North America unit cases declined 1%. This is a franchise holding revenue through pricing while physical throughput stalls. Unlike a Type B audit these are verified units rather than inferred from commentary, so confidence in the volume reading is High. The dominant finding is Stage 13: conversion has fallen from 115.2% to 40.4% over four years while operating margin held near 28.7%, and free cash flow more than halved from $11.3B to $5.3B. The market discusses Coca-Cola's pricing power, which is public and priced in; less than half of net income becoming cash is the part that is not.
thesis_breakers: Unit case volume turns negative worldwide for a full fiscal year, which under Type A rules is an automatic disqualification
FCF conversion remains below 50% for a third consecutive year, confirming structural rather than timing deterioration
Pricing power exhausts in the inflation-driven markets carrying price/mix, with Latin America's 11% contribution reversing
exit_criteria: Exit requires both conditions simultaneously: the market pricing a premium unmatched by peer operational metrics, AND no material year-over-year improvement in underlying growth. Given volume is already flat, the second condition is closer to being met. Any realized thesis-breaker triggers immediate exit review.
stages_missing: Stage 2.5 Normalized Earning Power - one-time items are not machine-extractable from XBRL
Stage 3 Distortion Stripping - fields are disclosed in KO's MD&A but not currently parsed into structured inputs
human_checks_outstanding: Provisional until at least two Stage 1 Primary Source Verification checks are complete. Priority: read the FY2024 and FY2025 cash flow statements and contingencies notes directly to establish what drove the conversion collapse. Also required: trade journal review of beverage volume trends, and outreach to bottling partners on North America sell-through. Research reasoning, not investment advice.
